name: ALEXANDRA MONROE
title: Senior Product Engineer
sloganHeroSection: I build products people love — from early-stage MVPs to systems at scale.
bio: I'm a product engineer with 8 years of experience across fintech, healthtech, and enterprise SaaS. I care deeply about the intersection of clean, scalable engineering and genuinely delightful user experience. When I'm not shipping, I'm mentoring junior engineers, contributing to open-source projects, and speaking at conferences about distributed systems and engineering leadership.

education.description: Graduated with Distinction — top 5% of class. Concentrated in distributed systems and human-computer interaction. Senior thesis on fault-tolerant consensus algorithms published in the ACM Digital Library.
achievements: Graduated with Distinction — top 5% of class
Senior thesis published in the ACM Digital Library
President of the Society of Women Engineers chapter

education.description: Self-directed research residency focused on building a distributed key-value store in Rust and studying consensus protocols including Raft and Paxos.
achievements: Built a distributed key-value store in Rust from scratch
Deep-dived Raft and Paxos consensus protocols
Published findings in the RC alumni newsletter

experience.description: Leading a cross-functional team of 6 engineers to rebuild the core payment infrastructure, cutting p99 latency by 40% and reducing operational costs by $2M annually. Shipped 3 major product lines that collectively onboarded 12,000 new enterprise customers.
highlights: Reduced p99 payment latency by 40% through infrastructure redesign
Shipped 3 product lines, onboarding 12,000+ enterprise customers
Mentored 4 junior engineers to senior-level promotions
Drove $2M in annual cost savings through infrastructure optimisation

experience.description: Built HIPAA-compliant patient intake flows in React and Node.js, integrating with Epic EHR to reduce patient onboarding time by 60%. Owned full-stack development of the telehealth module, which grew to serve 80,000 active patients.
highlights: Reduced patient onboarding time by 60% with new intake flows
Built telehealth module serving 80,000 active patients
Achieved HIPAA compliance certification across all patient-facing APIs
Maintained 99.99% uptime SLA across all patient-critical services

experience.description: Developed and maintained a white-label SaaS analytics platform serving 200+ B2B clients. Rebuilt the data ingestion pipeline from scratch, improving throughput 5x and enabling real-time dashboard updates.
highlights: Rebuilt data pipeline, improving throughput 5x for 200+ B2B clients
Reduced dashboard load time from 8s to under 1.2s
Delivered real-time dashboard updates across all client tiers
Owned white-label customisation system supporting 200+ brands

projects.title: ClearPath
projects.description: An open-source CLI tool for visualising dependency graphs in monorepos. Supports Nx, Turborepo, and pnpm workspaces. 2,800+ GitHub stars and adopted by engineering teams at Vercel, Linear, and Notion.

projects.title: Pulse Dashboard
projects.description: Real-time operations dashboard built for a Series B logistics startup. Handles 50k+ events per minute via WebSocket fan-out, with sub-100ms P99 update latency. Reduced on-call pages by 35% in the first quarter after launch.

projects.title: Formix
projects.description: Headless form-state library for React with zero-config validation, TypeScript-first API, and framework-agnostic adapters. 1,100+ stars, 12k weekly npm downloads, and zero production issues since v1.0.

projects.title: Meridian Design System
projects.description: An accessible, token-driven component library used across all Meridian Labs products. 60+ components, full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, and Figma-to-code synchronisation via Tokens Studio.

community.description: Maintainer of two npm packages — Formix and ClearPath — with 15k+ combined weekly downloads. Regularly reviews community PRs, triages issues, and writes migration guides for major releases.
Mentoring 30+ underrepresented students in tech across 3 cohorts. Conducts weekly 1:1 sessions, mock interviews, and resume reviews. Three mentees have since landed roles at FAANG companies.
Delivered talks at QCon San Francisco and NodeConf EU on scaling payment infrastructure and zero-downtime microservice migrations. Both sessions rated in the top 10% by attendees.

Led the San Francisco chapter of Women Who Code, growing membership from 120 to 800+ engineers over 3 years. Organised monthly workshops, hackathons, and career panels with speakers from Stripe, Airbnb, and Google.
